description.en: A dense, dark German pan loaf of wholegrain rye, seeds and sourdough. Almost no crumb holes, enormous flavor, it slices thin, keeps for a week and gets better by day three.
description.de: Ein dichtes, dunkles Kastenbrot aus Roggenvollkorn, Saaten und Sauerteig. Kaum Poren, dafür enorm viel Aroma, dünn aufgeschnitten, eine Woche haltbar, ab Tag drei am besten.
description.es: Un pan de molde alemán denso y oscuro de centeno integral, semillas y masa madre. Miga cerrada y sabor enorme: se corta fino, dura una semana y mejora al tercer día.
description.fr: Un pain moulé allemand dense et sombre au seigle complet, aux graines et au levain. Mie serrée, saveur immense, il se tranche finement, se garde une semaine et s'améliore dès le troisième jour.

origin.note: Protected term in Germany: Vollkornbrot must be at least 90% wholegrain.

keeping.note: Wrap in linen; wait 24h before the first slice or the crumb smears.

method.title: Sourdough & soaker
method.summary: The evening before: stir 200 g rye meal, 200 g water and 50 g starter into a sourdough; soak the flax seeds in 50 g water. Rest both overnight at room temperature (10–12 h).

method.title: Mix
method.summary: Combine sourdough, soaker, remaining flour and water, salt and toasted sunflower seeds into a heavy, clay-like paste. No kneading, rye has no gluten network to develop; just mix thoroughly.

method.title: Bulk rest
method.summary: Rest the dough 30–45 min covered so the meal fully hydrates.

method.title: Pan & proof
method.summary: Pack into a greased tin, smooth the top with wet hands, dust with rye and proof ~2 h at warm room temperature until small cracks show and it nears the rim.

method.title: Bake
method.summary: Bake at 220°C for 15 min, then 200°C for about 60 min to a core temperature of 96–98°C. Bold bake, the dark crust is flavor.

method.title: Wait
method.summary: Cool completely, then rest wrapped for a full day before slicing; the crumb needs it to set.

source.author: Lutz Geißler (style reference)
source.adaptation: Composite of classic German home-baking ratios; simplified one-stage sourdough.
